---
sidebar: true
toc: false
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
---

```{=html}
<!---
 ::: {.container-fluid} 
--->
```

```{=html}
<div class="p-5 mb-4 bg-light rounded-3 bgimg">
  <br><br><br><br>
  <div class="container-fluid py-5">
    <h1 class="display-5 fw-bold"> <br Berger></h1>
  </div>
</div>
<style type="text/css">
.lead {
    font-family: "Zilla Slab";
    text-align: center;
}

.bgimg {
  background-image: url('images/sep24.jpg');
background-size: 750px;
min-height: 506px;
background-repeat: no-repeat;
background-position: center top;
}
.hero-quote {
  font-family: "Zilla Slab";
  font-color: white;
  color: white;
}

.fw-bold {
  /* font-family: "Zilla Slab Highlight"; */
  font-style: bold !important;
  color: white !important;
}
</style>
```

```{=html}
<!---
 ::: 
--->
```

<br>

:::::: grid
::: g-col-1
:::

::: g-col-10
#### Research

Welcome!

My name is Julian Berger. I am an incoming Assistant Professor in the [Faculty of Economics and Business at Universitat Pompeu Fabra](https://www.upf.edu/en/web/econ). My position starts in September 2026.

Currently, I am a postdoctoral scholar at the University of Southern Denmark in the [Strategic and Organization Design](https://www.sdu.dk/en/forskning/forskningsenheder/samf/immsaml/forskningsgrupper/sod) group.

Previously, I was at the [Center for Adaptive Rationality, Max Planck Institute for Human Development](https://www.mpib-berlin.mpg.de/research/research-centers/adaptive-rationality) where I finished my PhD in April 2026. My PhD was funded by the [Foundation of German Business (Stiftung der deutschen Wirtschaft)](https://www.sdw.org/index.html). I have received further funding by the [Danish Data Science Adacemy](https://ddsa.dk/) and the [Joachim Herz Foundation](https://www.joachim-herz-stiftung.de/en/research/promotion-of-young-talents/add-on-fellowships-for-interdisciplinary-economics).

Generally, I work on improving the decision making of individuals, crowds and hybrid human-AI systems. I combine decision making research from psychology and economics with the latest insights from computer science. [You can find my projects here](projects/index.qmd) but [google scholar may be more up to date.](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=F3aVr0QAAAAJ&hl=en)

#### Industry

Together with Philip Jakob, I founded [RigorLabs](https://rigor.me/), a tech R&D firm that develops tool for improving the rigor and robustness of science.

During my PhD, I have also worked as a consultant and ML engineer. Here, my focus was on the development of interpretable machine learning models that are accurate, fair and transparent. It is good fun. In my industry work I often join forces with [Simply Rational](https://www.simplyrational.de/en/).
:::

::: g-col-1
:::
::::::
